text: Redis 6.2 cluster authentication relies on one shared requirepass password for every node in the cluster. The operational guidance describes a single password-based trust model across the cluster.

Redis 8 cluster authentication does not rely on one shared requirepass password for every node in the cluster. The operational guidance describes separate ACL identities and per-user permissions across the cluster.
Redis 8 keeps hot data in RAM and recommends sizing maxmemory with enough headroom for replication and persistence overhead.
